name: gh-fix-ci
description: Inspect GitHub PR checks with gh, pull failing GitHub Actions logs, summarize failure context, then create a fix plan and implement after user approval. Use when a user asks to debug or fix failing PR CI/CD checks on GitHub Actions and wants a plan + code changes; for external checks (e.g., Buildkite), only report the details URL and mark them out of scope.
capabilities: Inspect failing GitHub PR checks using gh CLI
Fetch and summarize GitHub Actions workflow logs
Identify actionable failure causes with context snippets
Create fix plans using the plan skill
Implement approved fixes and suggest re-running tests
author: davila7
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing SKILL.md metadata. References gh CLI for GitHub operations. The static scanner misidentified GitHub CLI commands as cryptographic algorithms and command documentation as shell execution. This is a legitimate DevOps workflow automation tool with no executable code. All 41 findings are false positives.
valueStatement: Failing CI/CD checks block PR merges and waste developer time. This skill inspects failing GitHub Actions workflows, extracts error logs, and creates a fix plan for your approval before implementing changes.
userTitle: Debug and fix failing GitHub CI checks
limitations: External CI providers (Buildkite, etc.) are out of scope
Requires gh CLI authentication with workflow/repo scopes
Cannot access private repos without proper permissions

useCases.title: Debug production CI failures
useCases.description: Quickly diagnose why production CI checks failed and get actionable fixes without manual log hunting

useCases.title: Fix your PR CI issues
useCases.description: Understand and resolve CI failures on your pull requests before requesting review

useCases.title: Review CI fix proposals
useCases.description: Get clear summaries of CI failures and approve proposed fixes before implementation

promptTemplates.title: Basic CI fix
promptTemplates.prompt: gh-fix-ci on this PR to check for failing CI jobs, summarize the errors, and create a plan to fix them.

promptTemplates.title: Specific PR debug
promptTemplates.prompt: Use gh-fix-ci to inspect PR #42, pull the failing GitHub Actions logs, and explain what needs to change.

promptTemplates.title: Log-focused analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Run gh-fix-ci on this repo with --max-lines 100 to get detailed log context for the failing test job.

promptTemplates.title: Full workflow fix
promptTemplates.prompt: gh-fix-ci on this PR: find all failing checks, summarize each failure, propose a fix plan, and apply the fixes after I approve.

bestPractices: Run gh auth login first to ensure proper authentication with workflow scopes
Provide the specific PR number or URL for faster resolution
Review the fix plan carefully before giving approval
antiPatterns: Running without gh CLI installed or authenticated
Skipping the approval step for risky code changes
Expecting support for non-GitHub CI providers

faq.question: Does this skill work with GitHub Enterprise?
faq.answer: Yes, if gh CLI is configured for your Enterprise instance. Ensure proper authentication before running.

faq.question: Can it fix Buildkite or CircleCI failures?
faq.answer: No, the skill only handles GitHub Actions. External CI failures are reported with a details URL but not fixed.

faq.question: What permissions does gh need?
faq.answer: gh requires repo and workflow scopes. Run 'gh auth login' then 'gh auth status' with escalated permissions.

faq.question: Is my GitHub token stored safely?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill uses gh CLI which stores credentials securely in your system keychain.

faq.question: Why cannot it access some workflow logs?
faq.answer: Logs may be expired, deleted, or require additional permissions. The skill will report missing logs explicitly.

faq.question: How does this compare to GitHub own debugging?
faq.answer: This skill automates log retrieval and error analysis, saving time hunting through the GitHub web interface.
